scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector uses Mechanism (ii): inspector-side tagging. Every finding includes introduced_in_diff: bool. Only findings with introduced_in_diff: true are eligible to block close per AC-2.6(b). Files outside commands/*.md and agents/*.md are classified as out-of-scope for verbosity detection; findings on such files are advisory only.

file_analysis.severity_note: 24 verbose lines / 211 total = 11.4%. Minor threshold requires >= 50 verbose lines OR 15% verbosity_percentage. Both thresholds not met. Codex CF-1 correctly identified that the original ~40-line draft figure at ~19% would have crossed the major percentage threshold (>=15%), but after applying CF-2/CF-3/CF-4 false-positive corrections the true concision-candidate lines are ~24 at 11.4% — below all thresholds.

observations_sub_threshold.location: lines 150-160 (Step 4 'for reference' grant field list, ~11 lines)
observations_sub_threshold.verdict: Below minor threshold. The 11-line field list documents what execute-push.py validates internally. The orchestrator does not perform this validation; it could be compressed to: 'Do not manually read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant. execute-push.py performs grant validation, grant consumption, Chain-B sentinel write, and push.sh exec atomically.' Not required for cycle close.

Below minor threshold. The why-rationale for single-process exec is a sub-threshold concision candidate. Lines 167-171 (WARNING blockquote) are load-bearing rules and MUST be preserved. The rationale prose at 173-180 can be compressed to: 'execute-push.py validates/consumes the push-analyst grant, writes the Chain-B sentinel, and execs push.sh in the same process so the <=60s sentinel gate is preserved.' Not required for cycle close.

observations_sub_threshold.location: lines 198-202 (Push-analyst grant TTL history rationale, ~5 lines)
observations_sub_threshold.verdict: Below minor threshold. The 180s TTL constant and reference to agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 are functional rules. The 'raised from 120 to 180 in task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4' historical rationale is sub-threshold prose. Noted also in prior cycle's report (prompt-inspector-report-20260519-211515-redev9items.json, observations_sub_threshold entry for push.md TTL). Not required for cycle close.

Below minor threshold. The preamble mostly contains load-bearing behavioral rules (disable-model-invocation scope, mandatory push-analyst gate, no-bounce constraint). Could be tightened slightly. Codex CF-5 flagged this OBSERVATION_ONLY. Not required for cycle close.

file_analysis.recommendation: No reportable minor+ verbosity violations. Four sub-threshold concision observations logged. All are advisory and non-blocking. The Agentic dispatch protocol section (105 lines) is primarily substantive procedural rules; only ~24 lines across three sub-locations are pure concision candidates, well below the 50-line minor threshold.

file_analysis.verdict: OUT OF SCOPE — Python implementation script. The verbosity detection rules apply to command/agent documentation files (*.md) in commands/ and agents/ directories. The module docstring at lines 1-31 is a Python module docstring — implementation documentation embedded in source code, not a slash-command prompt document. Confirmed by codex CF-6 (OBSERVATION_ONLY: 'Keep it out of prompt-verbosity findings; Python source/docstrings are not command/agent Markdown prompt docs').

summary.verdict: CLEAN — zero reportable minor+ verbosity violations. commands/push.md has 4 sub-threshold concision observations (total ~24 concision-candidate lines / 211 = 11.4%, below both the 50-line and 15% minor thresholds after codex false-positive corrections). scripts/execute-push.py is out of scope. No introduced_in_diff: true findings exist; close is not blocked by this inspector.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 items (5 with PROPOSED_FIX, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY). Key corrections applied: (CF-1) severity reclassification — original ~40-line aggregate at ~19% would have been major by percentage, but after CF-2/CF-3/CF-4 false-positive corrections true concision candidates are ~24 lines at 11.4%, below all thresholds; (CF-2) Step 5 WARNING blockquote is load-bearing rules not why-prose — narrowed finding to rationale-only lines 173-180; (CF-3) TTL section contains functional TTL=180 rule and cross-reference — narrowed to history rationale paragraph only; (CF-4) Step 4 finding narrowed to lines 150-160 only, preserving lines 146-148 and 162-163 as procedural constraints; (CF-5) preamble observation added OBSERVATION_ONLY; (CF-6) execute-push.py out-of-scope conclusion confirmed OBSERVATION_ONLY.

findings.issue: Severity misclassified: ~40 lines at ~19% verbosity_percentage maps to 'major' by percentage threshold (15-30%), not 'minor'
findings.proposed_fix: Reclassify commands/push.md as findings=[]; observations_sub_threshold=[Step 4 field-list, Step 5 partial rationale, TTL rationale]. If insisting on ~40-line aggregate, severity must be major not minor.
findings.rationale: Applied by incorporating CF-2/CF-3/CF-4 false-positive corrections which reduce true concision-candidate lines from ~40 to ~24 (11.4%), below both minor thresholds. findings[] stays empty.

findings.issue: Step 5 over-flagged as why-prose: lines 167-171 (WARNING blockquote) are load-bearing rules (no-subagent mandate, direct-execution constraint); lines 173-180 are the actual rationale prose concision candidates
findings.proposed_fix: Partial concision observation only: compress rationale prose (173-180), preserve WARNING rule block (167-171). Suggested: 'The orchestrator MUST run execute-push.py directly; do NOT delegate Steps 4-5. execute-push.py validates/consumes the push-analyst grant, writes the Chain-B sentinel, and execs push.sh in the same process so the <=60s sentinel gate is preserved.'
findings.rationale: Applied: Step 5 finding narrowed from ~14 lines to ~8 lines (173-180 only); WARNING blockquote classified as load-bearing rule.

findings.issue: TTL section over-classified as entirely rationale/history prose: TTL=180 constant, agents/push-analyst.md reference, and commit-grant distinction are functional rules; only the 'raised from 120...' history paragraph is a concision candidate
findings.proposed_fix: Replace section with: 'Push-analyst Chain-B grants use PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180, defined in agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7. This is separate from scripts/write-commit-grant.py GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10.'
findings.rationale: Applied: TTL section finding narrowed from 14 lines to ~5 lines (history rationale only, lines 198-202). Functional rule portions preserved.

findings.issue: Step 4 finding should be narrowed: lines 146-148 ('orchestrator MUST NOT read/validate/unlink') and 162-163 ('applies verdict logic, consumes grant, writes sentinel, execs push.sh') are procedural constraints; only lines 150-160 are the 'for reference' field list concision candidate
findings.proposed_fix: Report only lines 150-160 as optional compression. Suggested wording: 'Do not manually read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant. execute-push.py performs grant validation, grant consumption, Chain-B sentinel write, and push.sh exec atomically.'
findings.rationale: Applied: Step 4 finding already scoped to lines 150-160 in draft; codex confirms this scoping is correct. Finding remains as sub-threshold concision observation.

findings.issue: Missed sub-threshold concision observation in opening preamble (lines 8-20): repeats guard/dispatch context before formal protocol
findings.proposed_fix: Mark OBSERVATION_ONLY. Suggested: '/push publishes the current branch through ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh after mandatory push-analyst approval. disable-model-invocation: true blocks SlashCommand self-dispatch only; when user-invoked, execute Steps 0-5 below. Do not run push.sh before analyst validation, and do not bounce wrapper execution back to the user.'
findings.rationale: Added as fourth sub-threshold observation. Preamble content is mostly load-bearing (behavioral scope of disable-model-invocation, mandatory analyst gate, no-bounce rule). OBSERVATION_ONLY per codex.

findings.issue: execute-push.py out-of-scope conclusion is correct — Python source/docstrings are not command/agent Markdown prompt docs
